title: Redesign Summary Cards on Metric Analyst Results Screen
story: As a DataTools Pro user running the Metrics Analyst, I want the summary cards to display clean iconography with clear labels for each metric category, so that I can quickly understand the analysis results at a glance.
description:
The summary cards displayed after Metrics Analyst completes need visual improvements. The cards should use Bootstrap icons for visual meaning, display the metric label (e.g., 'Reports Processed'), a count value, and a 'More info' action link. A reusable CSS class should be created for these cards so they can be used in other parts of the app where data is pre-processed or summarized.

The design reads left to right: icon for visual context, the report label tells you what, the count tells how much, and the action button in the lower right lets you drill in.
acceptance_criteria: Each summary card displays a Bootstrap icon, a label, a count, and a 'More info' link
Cards use a consistent, reusable CSS class for metric analysis cards
Cards are styled with a clean blue color scheme instead of red for error states
Layout reads left-to-right: icon, label, count, action
ux_changes: Redesigned Metrics Analyst summary cards with Bootstrap iconography, consistent styling, and a left-to-right layout (icon, label, count, action). Created a reusable card CSS class.
implementation_notes: Cards leverage the existing Bootstrap icon library already loaded in the app. A new reusable CSS class was created so these card styles can be applied in other data summary contexts.
comment_summary: Developer updated the cards on staging for review.
